Intercont (Cayman) Ltd prices IPO at $7/share, offering 1.5M Ordinary Shares, expecting $10.5M gross proceeds, to trade on Nasdaq as "NCT".
news.summary: Intercont (Cayman) Limited, a global shipping company venturing into seaborne pulping operations, has disclosed the pricing details of its initial public offering (IPO) on March 27, 2025. The offering consists of 1,500,000 ordinary shares valued at $7.00 per share. This pricing is anticipated to raise $10,500,000 in total gross proceeds for the company, excluding underwriting discounts and other expenses related to the offering. The Ordinary Shares have received approval for listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market and are set to start trading on March 28, 2025, under the symbol “NCT”.

news.summary: According to recent data, the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in over a decade. Approximately 200,000 new jobs were created last month, exceeding economists' expectations. This marks a significant improvement in the labor market compared to previous months. The total number of unemployed individuals stands at 6.8 million, a decrease of 300,000 from the previous month. Job growth was particularly strong in the healthcare and technology sectors, with notable increases in manufacturing and construction as well. The decrease in unemployment and the rise in job creation are positive indicators for the country's economy, suggesting a trend towards recovery and stability.

news.headline: Reed's reports $9.7M net sales in Q4 2024, operating loss improves to $3.7M, and FY 2024 net sales are $38M, with an operating loss of $8.1M. Management focused on balance sheet improvement and launching new products.
news.summary: Reed's, Inc. has issued a corrected earnings press release for Q4 and FY 2024, adjusting for a $750,000 provision related to contract proceedings with a vendor. In Q4 2024, net sales were $9.7 million (down from $11.7 million in Q4 2023), with gross profit at $2.9 million (compared to $0.5 million in Q4 2013). Operating loss improved to $3.7 million from $4.9 million. For FY 2024, net sales were $38.0 million (compared to $44.7 million in FY 2013), with gross profit at $11.4 million (up from $9.7 million in FY 2013). Operating loss improved to $8.1 million from $9.4 million. The company's Modified EBITDA was $(4.1) million for FY 2024. Reed's CEO, Norman E. Snyder, Jr., highlighted progress made in 2024 and strategic steps taken to drive growth and profitability. The company saw improvements in gross profit, operating loss, and aims to expand product portfolio and retail presence. Total debt net of capitalized fees stood at $9.6 million as of Dec 31, 2024, compared to $27.4 million in 2013. Reed's looks forward to continued growth in 2025, focusing on efficiency and scalability.

news.headline: Reed's corrects Q4 & FY 2024 results, includes $750,000 provision for vendor proceedings. Operating loss impacted.
news.summary: Norwalk, Connecticut, on March 27, 2025 - Following the identification of a $750,000 provision linked to contract proceedings with a vendor, The Company has released an amended version of its fourth quarter and full year 2024 earnings press release. This adjustment has impacted the previously reported figures for selling, general, and administrative expenses as well as the operating loss.

news.headline: Sprouts Farmers Market partners with 8 female college athletes, expanding NIL deals for women's sports. #EmpoweringWomen
news.summary: In Phoenix, Sprouts demonstrates its dedication to the PowHERed by Sprouts initiative by entering into eight new Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) agreements with female college athletes.

news.headline: US DOJ seized $201,400 in crypto linked to Hamas for terrorism financing. Regulators urge more supervision for crypto industry.
news.summary: The US Justice Department revealed the seizure of over $200,000 in cryptocurrency linked to Hamas in March. The funds were traced to Hamas controlled addresses and used to launder $1.5 million in digital assets since October 2024. A total of at least 17 wallets hold the seized funds. In response to Hamas's crypto activities, the US Treasury and its partners imposed sanctions on networks associated with Hamas in January 2024. Binance, a major exchange, faced legal action for allegedly aiding terrorists, claiming they had no partnership with Hamas. Concerns over money laundering in crypto have prompted questions about the need for stricter regulations. While terrorism financing through crypto remains low, the industry's vulnerabilities have been highlighted through incidents like the Hamas-related seizure.

news.headline: Bar Louie reaches agreement with lender to reduce debt through Chapter 11 for 31 corporate locations, operations to continue.
news.summary: In Dallas on March 27, 2025, Bar Louie disclosed that it has successfully secured an agreement with its lender to lower debt and provide backing for the company amidst a Chapter 11 restructuring strategy for its 31 corporate-owned establishments. The operations will persist without any disruptions.

news.headline: NZ Development West Coast Invests AU$3M in Federation Mining
Eagle Eye Networks introduces Eagle Eye Automations for proactive security, empowering businesses to automate tasks and improve operations.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas, Eagle Eye Networks, a top provider of cloud video surveillance, has introduced Eagle Eye Automations. This new offering is a versatile rules engine designed to automate tasks, allowing businesses to enhance security, safety, and operations cost-effectively. With today's security setups becoming more intricate by integrating various technologies like video surveillance, AI, analytics, and sensors, Eagle Eye Automations provides a solution to streamline these disparate systems.

news.headline: Global Atomic reported operating & financial results for 2024, with Dasa Project progressing towards 2026 production start-up. US$375.6M estimated capital costs, increasing to US$392.8M. Spot Uranium prices ranged from $65 to $120/pound U3O8 in 2024. Turkish JV showed improved financials, cash balance of $4.4M at year-end. Dasa Project achievements include reaching new uranium deposits & advancing plant construction. Management in talks for debt financing with a U.S. Development Bank. Company raised $20M in private placement in July 2024 and closed a $40.25M public offering in October 2024. Ending cash balance as of December 31, 2024, was $18.7M. President and CEO disclosed plans for flexible spending until securing financing. Projecting readying production in H1 2026. Global Atomic retained efforts for local engagement and government support in Niger.@Service.
news.summary: Global Atomic Corporation reported its operating and financial results for 2024, focusing on the Dasa Uranium Project's progress. The project is slated for a Q1 2026 production startup, with Dasa offering significant employment and social benefits in the Niger region. The company is seeking financing through a U.S. Development Bank and potential joint venture partnerships, with capital costs estimated at $392.8 million. Uranium prices fluctuated in 2024, with spot prices ranging from $65 to $120 per pound U3O8. Additionally, the Turkish Zinc Joint-Venture saw profitability in 2025. The Company's cash balance stood at $18.7 million as of December 31, 2024.

news.headline: Rocket Lab's Neutron rocket chosen by US Space Force for $5.6 billion NSSL program, competing in Phase 3 Lane 1.
news.summary: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. has been chosen by the U.S. Space Force to vie for the Department of Defense’s crucial national security missions under the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 program. This selection allows Rocket Lab to compete in a firm-fixed price, indefinite delivery/indefinite-quantity contract spanning five years, with an ordering period through to. Rocket Lab, a leading company in launch services and space systems, operates under the Nasdaq symbol RKLB.

news.headline: Investors in Fluence Energy (NASDAQ: FLNC) have until May 12, 2025, to join a securities class action lawsuit.
news.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been initiated by Berger Montague PC against Fluence Energy, Inc. ("Fluence" or the "Company") (NASDAQ: FLNC) on behalf of investors who acquired Fluence securities from November 29, 2023, through an unspecified date in 2025, impacting a significant number of investors. The lawsuit alleges certain violations by Fluence during the stated period, urging affected investors to take necessary steps in the legal process.

news.headline: Empire Petroleum reports Q4-2024 net production of 2,356 Boe/d, with assets in multiple states. Year-end 2024 proved reserves disclosed.
news.summary: Empire Petroleum, a leading oil and gas company listed on NYSE American (EP), disclosed its operational and financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year 2024. The company, which has production assets spread across New Mexico, North Dakota, Montana, Texas, and Louisiana, reported significant achievements. In the fourth quarter of 2024, Empire achieved net production volumes of 2,356 barrels of oil equivalent per day (Boe/d), with 1,581 barrels of oil included. These figures demonstrate the company's robust production capabilities and highlight a strong finish to the year. Additionally, Empire disclosed its year-end 2024 proved reserves, solidifying its position in the market.

news.headline: Nutex Health reschedules earnings release and conference call for Q4 and full year 2024 ending Dec 31.
news.summary: On March 27, 2025, healthcare company Nutex Health Inc. (NASDAQ: NUTX) disclosed a rescheduling of the release of its financial results and conference call for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The new date for the announcement was not specified in the report.

news.headline: VitalHub's 2024 results show significant growth: ARR up 59% to $71.1M, Revenue up 51% to $20.6M, Adjusted EBITDA up 27% to $5.0M.
news.summary: The company's Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) experienced a significant annual increase of 59%, reaching $71.1 million. Total Revenue also saw a notable uptick, rising by 51% year over year to $20.6 million. Additionally, the Adjusted EBITDA surged by 27% year over year to reach $5.0 million.

FlexGen introduces new State of Health feature in HybridOS to monitor battery degradation, enhancing efficiency.
news.summary: FlexGen, a company based in Durham, North Carolina, has introduced a new update to their HybridOS software. This update includes an improved State of Health feature, providing customers with a more transparent and dependable insight into battery degradation. This enhancement aims to offer users crucial information on the condition of their batteries, ensuring better decision-making regarding their energy storage systems. This development by FlexGen highlights their commitment to delivering advanced solutions to their customers.

Crypto currently resembles the Internet's 'AOL era', says Sandeep Nailwal. 10-15 years needed for full glory. Only 4% worldwide own BTC.
news.summary: Polygon co-founder Sandeep Nailwal compared the current state of crypto to the early days of the internet's AOL era in the late 1990s, highlighting clunky user experiences and limited use cases. Nailwal outlined key areas for improvement, including fiat on- and off-ramps, custody solutions, and hardware wallets on mobile devices. He predicted it would take at least another 10-15 years for crypto to reach its full potential. The internet took 30-40 years to achieve mass adoption, starting with limited use cases. Today, financial use cases dominate crypto, but Nailwal believes adoption will expand to alternative sectors like social media and gaming. Only 4% of individuals globally owned Bitcoin as of February 2025, indicating mainstream adoption is still in its early stages. The industry is currently in the early adopter phase, and crypto's full potential has yet to be realized.

Dozens of military families in Key West allege fraud over toxic housing conditions at NAS.
news.summary: Court claims have been filed in Miami stating that defendants failed to disclose "dangerous, horrific" living conditions at Naval Air Station (NAS) Key West. Numerous military families were impacted by toxic privatized housing. The claims allege fraud and gross negligence, with over 50 households affected. The news was released on March 27, 2025.
